titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 554

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=13 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:19:35.804054+00 | translation moa=14 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:19:35.812898+00

div.p:
Ego dunecanus filius regis Malcolumb constans hereditarię
rex scotię  dedi in elemosina Sancto Cuthberto & suis seruitoribus Tininge
ham . Aldeham . Scuchale . Cnolle . Hatheruuich . & de Broccesmuthe
omne seruitium quod inde habuit Fodanus episcopus . & hęc dedi in tali quitan
tia cum saca & soca . qualem unquam meliorem habuit sanctus Cuthbertus .
ab illis de quibus tenet suas elemosinas . Et hoc dedi pro me ipso . &
pro anima patris mei . & pro fratribus meis . & pro uxore mea . & pro infan
tibus meis . Et quoniam uolui quod istud donum stabile esset . sancto cuthberto 
feci quod fratres mei concesserunt . Qui autem istud uoluerit destruere .
uel ministris sancti cuthberti aliquid inde auferre  maledictionem dei .
et sancti Cuthberti . & meam  habeat . amen. Crux dun+ecani regis scribtoris gr+entonis
ace+ard ul+f malcolumb + eadgari +
her+mer aelf+ric vui+get
hem+ming teo+dbold ea+rnulf

p.cl: I, Duncan, son of King Malcolm, by inheritance undoubted king of Scotland,

have given in alms to Saint Cuthbert and his own servants, Tyninghame, Auldhame, Scoughall, Knowes, Hedderwick, and at Broxmouth all the service that Bishop Fothad had from that place, and I have given these in such quittance with sake and soke, as better than Saint Cuthbert ever had from those of whom he holds his own alms. And I have given this for myself and for my father's soul, and for my brothers, and for my wife, and for my children. And since I have willed that this gift should permanently belong to Saint Cuthbert, I have done what my brothers have allowed.
But let the one who would destroy this thing, or take anything of it away from the servants of Saint Cuthbert, have God’s curse, and Saint Cuthbert’s, and mine. Amen.
The cross of King Dun+can. Of the scribe, Gr+ent. Ach+ard. Ul+f. Malcolm+. Edgar+. Her+mer. Ælf+ric. Uv+iet. Hem+ming. Theo+bald. E+rnulf
